# Plakar

> Plakar is an open-source backup tool that provides end-to-end encrypted,
> deduplicated backups. It is powered by Kloset, an immutable data store engine
> that handles chunking, deduplication, compression, and encryption, and
> represents all backed-up data internally as a virtual filesystem. Plakar's
> integration model allows any data source, storage backend, or restore
> destination to be supported through pluggable connectors, making it adaptable
> to files, databases, object storage, SaaS, and more. It is designed for a
> minimal memory footprint and scales to petabyte-scale infrastructure. Plakar
> Control Plane (PCP) is the enterprise product built on top of Plakar.

## Overview

- [Quick Start](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/quickstart/first-backup): Quick introduction to installing Plakar and running your first backup.
- [How Plakar Works](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/how-plakar-works): Architectural overview of Plakar and Kloset.
- [What is Kloset](https://www.plakar.io/posts/2025-04-29/kloset-the-immutable-data-store): Kloset's immutable storage model, chunking, encryption, and snapshot architecture.
- [What is Plakar Control Plane](https://www.plakar.io/control-plane-docs/intro/overview): Overview of PCP, the enterprise product built on top of Plakar.

## Plakar Community Docs

Plakar Community version is open-source and free to use. Documentation is versioned to match releases. Replace the version segment in any `/docs/` URL to switch versions. Only use these four version identifiers — no others exist.

- [main (development)](https://www.plakar.io/docs/main): Bleeding-edge branch, may be unstable.
- [v1.1.0 (latest stable)](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0): Latest stable release, recommended for all users.
- [v1.0.6](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.0.6): Previous stable release.
- [v1.0.5](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.0.5): Previous stable release.

### Getting Started

- [Installation](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/quickstart/installation): How to install Plakar on your system.
- [First Backup](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/quickstart/first-backup): End-to-end walkthrough: create a Kloset store, run a backup, list snapshots, verify integrity, and restore.
- [Synchronize Kloset stores](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/quickstart/synchronize-copies): Duplicate a Kloset store to another location using `plakar sync`.
- [Backup Non-Filesystem Data](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/quickstart/backup-non-filesystem): Back up an S3 bucket using a configured source, and restore it to an S3 destination.

### Guides

- [Create a Kloset Repository](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/create-kloset-repository): Creating and configuring a Kloset store.
- [Importing Configurations](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/importing-configurations): How to import configurations for stores, sources, and destinations in Plakar.
- [Serve a Kloset Store Over the Network](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/serving-a-kloset-store-over-the-network): Expose a Kloset store over the network to allow access from other machines.
- [Exclude Files from a Backup](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/excluding-files-from-a-backup): Use exclusion patterns to skip files or directories during backup.
- [Retrieve Passphrase via External Command](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/retrieve-passphrase-kloset-store): Delegate Kloset store passphrase retrieval to an external command or secret manager.
- [Creating a Custom Connector](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/creating-a-custom-connector): Build and package your own data source connector (integration).
- [Log In to Plakar](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/logging-in-to-plakar): Log in to unlock pre-built integration packages and alerts.
- [Managing Packages](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/managing-packages): Install, build, update and remove Plakar integrations.
- [Using Plakar Prune](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/using-plakar-prune): Remove old snapshots and reclaim storage space.
- [Using Plakar UI](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/using-plakar-ui): Using the web interface to browse snapshots, restore files, install integrations, etc.
- [MySQL: Physical Backups](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/mysql/physical-backups): Back up raw MySQL data directories using physical backup method.
- [MySQL: SQL Dump](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/mysql/sqldump): Logical SQL dump backup for MySQL and MariaDB using the MySQL integration.
- [PostgreSQL: pg_basebackup](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/postgres/pg-base-backup): Full physical cluster backup for PostgreSQL using the PostgreSQL integration.
- [PostgreSQL: pg_dump](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/postgres/pgdump): Logical SQL dump backup for PostgreSQL using the PostgreSQL integration.
- [Exoscale: Backup Managed MySQL](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/exoscale/backup-exoscale-managed-mysql): Back up a MySQL database hosted on Exoscale.
- [Exoscale: Dedicated Backup Server](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/exoscale/exoscale-compute-as-a-dedicated-backup-server): Use an Exoscale compute as a centralized backup server.
- [OVHcloud: Backup Managed PostgreSQL](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/ovhcloud/backup-ovhcloud-managed-postgres): Back up a PostgreSQL database hosted on OVHcloud.
- [OVHcloud: Dedicated Backup Server](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/guides/ovhcloud/ovhcloud-as-a-dedicated-backup-server): Use an OVHcloud VPS as a centralized backup server.

### Explanations

- [Should You Pull or Push Backups](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/should-you-pull-or-push-backups): How to decide between push and pull backup architectures, and how Plakar supports both.
- [How Many Kloset Stores Should I Have](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/how-many-kloset-stores): How to decide between a single shared store or multiple stores based on deduplication boundaries, encryption scope, and operational needs.
- [Why Several Copies](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/why-several-copies): Why multiple backup copies matter and the 3-2-1 backup strategy.
- [Why Should I Back Up My SaaS](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/why-should-i-backup-my-saas): Why SaaS availability is not the same as data protection, and why independent backups are necessary under the shared responsibility model.
- [How Maintenance Works](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/explanations/how-maintenance-works): How Plakar reclaims storage after snapshot deletion: chunking, packfiles, garbage collection, and the grace period.

### Integrations

- [S3](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/s3): Back up to, from, and restore to any S3-compatible object storage
- [SFTP](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/sftp): Back up remote hosts over SSH/SFTP.

### Integrations

Each integration tells Plakar how to interact with a specific storage system in up to three ways: as a **source** (read data to back up), as a **store** (host a Kloset store), or as a **destination** (restore data back to that system).

- [S3](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/s3): S3-compatible object storage (store, source, destination).
- [SFTP](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/sftp): SFTP/SSH-accessible servers (store, source, destination).
- [HTTP(S)](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/https): HTTP/HTTPS endpoint for stores exposed by `plakar server` (store).
- [Notion](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/notion): Notion workspace via the Notion API (source, destination).
- [Dropbox](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/dropbox): Dropbox via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [iCloud Drive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/icloud-drive): iCloud Drive via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [Koofr](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/koofr): Koofr via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [Google Drive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/google-drive): Google Drive via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [OneDrive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/onedrive): OneDrive via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [OpenDrive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/opendrive): OpenDrive via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [Proton Drive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/proton-drive): Proton Drive via Rclone (store, source, destination).
- [Proxmox](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/proxmox): Proxmox VMs and containers via vzdump (source, destination).
- [Kubernetes](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/kubernetes): Kubernetes cluster resources, manifests, and persistent volumes (source).
- [etcd](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/integrations/etcd): etcd cluster snapshots (source).

### Commands

- [plakar archive](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-archive): Create an archive from a Plakar snapshot.
- [plakar backup](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-backup): Create a new snapshot in a Kloset store.
- [plakar cat](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-cat): Display file contents from a Plakar snapshot.
- [plakar check](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-check): Check data integrity in a Kloset repository.
- [plakar create](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-create): Create a new Kloset repository.
- [plakar destination](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-destination): Manage Plakar restore destination configuration.
- [plakar diag](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-diag): Display detailed information about Plakar internal structures.
- [plakar diff](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-diff): Show differences between files in a Plakar snapshots.
- [plakar digest](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-digest): Compute digests for files in a Plakar snapshot.
- [plakar dup](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-dup): Duplicates an existing snapshot with a different ID.
- [plakar info](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-info): Display detailed information about internal structures.
- [plakar locate](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-locate): Find filenames in a Plakar snapshot.
- [plakar login](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-login): Authenticate to Plakar services.
- [plakar logout](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-logout): Log out from Plakar services.
- [plakar ls](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-ls): List snapshots and their contents in a Kloset repository.
- [plakar maintenance](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-maintenance): Remove unused data from a Plakar repository.
- [plakar mount](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-mount): Mount Plakar snapshots as read-only filesystem.
- [plakar pkg add](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-add): Install Plakar plugins.
- [plakar pkg build](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-build): Build Plakar plugins from source.
- [plakar pkg create](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-create): Package a plugin.
- [plakar pkg manifest](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-manifest): Manifest for plugin assemblation.
- [plakar pkg recipe](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-recipe): Recipe to build Plakar plugins from source.
- [plakar pkg rm](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-rm): Uninstall Plakar plugins.
- [plakar pkg show](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-pkg-show): Show installed Plakar plugins.
- [plakar policy](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-policy): Manage Plakar retention policies.
- [plakar prune](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-prune): Prune snapshots according to a policy.
- [plakar ptar](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-ptar): Generate a self-contained Kloset archive (.ptar).
- [plakar query](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-query): Query flags shared among many Plakar subcommands.
- [plakar restore](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-restore): Restore files from a Plakar snapshot.
- [plakar rm](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-rm): Remove snapshots from a Plakar repository.
- [plakar server](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-server): Start a Plakar server.
- [plakar server](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-server): Manage optional Plakar-connected services.
- [plakar source](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-source): Manage Plakar backup source configurationn.
- [plakar store](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-store): Manage Plakar store configurations.
- [plakar sync](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-sync): Synchronize snapshots between Plakar repositories.
- [plakar token create](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-token-create): Create a token to authenticate to Plakar services.
- [plakar ui](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-ui): Serve the Plakar web user interface.
- [plakar version](https://www.plakar.io/docs/v1.1.0/references/commands/plakar-version): Display the current Plakar version.
